  48: 
  49: func process() -> void:
  50: 	if overlay == null || !is_instance_valid(overlay):
  51: 		return
  52: 	if !overlay.has_method("is_enabled") or !overlay.is_enabled():
  53: 		return
  54: 	if world_sync == null:
  55: 		return
  56: 	if latest_gameplay_state.is_empty():
  57: 		if !_logged_waiting_for_gameplay_state:
  58: 			_logged_waiting_for_gameplay_state = true
  59: 			Log.world_sync_debug("server hitbox overlay waiting for gameplay state")
  60: 		return
  61: 	if shape_catalog_store == null or shape_catalog_store.shape_count() == 0:
  62: 		if !_logged_waiting_for_shape_catalog:
  63: 			_logged_waiting_for_shape_catalog = true
  64: 			Log.world_sync_debug("server hitbox overlay waiting for shape catalog")
  65: 		return
  66: 
  67: 	var draw_entries: Array = []
  68: 	var world_value = latest_gameplay_state.get("world", {})
  69: 	var players_value = world_value.get("ships", {}) if world_value is Dictionary else {}
  70: 	if players_value is Dictionary:
  71: 		for player_id in players_value:
  72: 			var player_state_value = players_value[player_id]
  73: 			if !(player_state_value is Dictionary):
  74: 				continue
  75: 
  76: 			var shape_id = DebugShapeIdResolver.player_shape_id(player_state_value)
  77: 			var shape_definition = shape_catalog_store.shape_for_id(shape_id) if shape_catalog_store != null else {}
  78: 			if shape_definition.is_empty():
  79: 				continue
  80: 
  81: 			var x = float(player_state_value.get("x", 0.0))
  82: 			var y = float(player_state_value.get("y", 0.0))
  83: 			var rotation = float(player_state_value.get("rotation", 0.0))
  84: 			var points = _shape_definition_visual_points(shape_definition, x, y, rotation, 1.0)
  85: 			if points.is_empty():
  86: 				continue
  87: 
  88: 			draw_entries.append({
  89: 				"kind": "player",
  90: 				"id": shape_id,
  91: 				"points": points,
  92: 			})
  93: 
  94: 	var asteroids_value = world_value.get("asteroids", {}) if world_value is Dictionary else {}
  95: 	if asteroids_value is Dictionary:
  96: 		for asteroid_id in asteroids_value:
  97: 			var asteroid_state_value = asteroids_value[asteroid_id]
  98: 			if !(asteroid_state_value is Dictionary):
  99: 				continue
 100: 
 101: 			var shape_id = DebugShapeIdResolver.asteroid_shape_id(asteroid_state_value)
 102: 			var shape_definition = shape_catalog_store.shape_for_id(shape_id) if shape_catalog_store != null else {}
 103: 			if shape_definition.is_empty():
 104: 				shape_id = "asteroid:0"
 105: 				shape_definition = shape_catalog_store.shape_for_id(shape_id) if shape_catalog_store != null else {}
 106: 			if shape_definition.is_empty():
 107: 				continue
 108: 
 109: 			var x = float(asteroid_state_value.get("x", 0.0))
 110: 			var y = float(asteroid_state_value.get("y", 0.0))
 111: 			var rotation = float(asteroid_state_value.get("rotation", 0.0))
 112: 			var scale = float(asteroid_state_value.get("scale", 1.0))
 113: 			var points = _shape_definition_visual_points(shape_definition, x, y, rotation, scale)
 114: 			if points.is_empty():
 115: 				continue
 116: 
 117: 			draw_entries.append({
 118: 				"kind": "asteroid",
 119: 				"id": shape_id,
 120: 				"points": points,
 121: 			})
 122: 
 123: 	var bullets_value = world_value.get("bullets", {}) if world_value is Dictionary else {}
 124: 	if bullets_value is Dictionary:
 125: 		for bullet_id in bullets_value:
 126: 			var bullet_state_value = bullets_value[bullet_id]
 127: 			if !(bullet_state_value is Dictionary):
 128: 				continue
 129: 
 130: 			var shape_id = DebugShapeIdResolver.bullet_shape_id(bullet_state_value)
 131: 			var shape_definition = shape_catalog_store.shape_for_id(shape_id) if shape_catalog_store != null else {}
 132: 			if shape_definition.is_empty():
 133: 				continue
 134: 
 135: 			var x = float(bullet_state_value.get("x", 0.0))
 136: 			var y = float(bullet_state_value.get("y", 0.0))
 137: 			var rotation = float(bullet_state_value.get("rotation", 0.0))
 138: 			var points = _shape_definition_visual_points(shape_definition, x, y, rotation, 1.0)
 139: 			if points.is_empty():
 140: 				continue
 141: 
 142: 			draw_entries.append({
 143: 				"kind": "bullet",
 144: 				"id": shape_id,
 145: 				"points": points,
 146: 			})
 147: 
 148: 	var pickups_value = world_value.get("pickups", {}) if world_value is Dictionary else {}
 149: 	if pickups_value is Dictionary:
 150: 		for pickup_id in pickups_value:
 151: 			var pickup_state_value = pickups_value[pickup_id]
 152: 			if !(pickup_state_value is Dictionary):
 153: 				continue
 154: 
 155: 			var shape_id = DebugShapeIdResolver.pickup_shape_id(pickup_state_value)
 156: 			var shape_definition = shape_catalog_store.shape_for_id(shape_id) if shape_catalog_store != null else {}
 157: 			if shape_definition.is_empty():
 158: 				continue
 159: 
 160: 			var x = float(pickup_state_value.get("x", 0.0))
 161: 			var y = float(pickup_state_value.get("y", 0.0))
 162: 			var points = _shape_definition_visual_points(shape_definition, x, y, 0.0, 1.0)
 163: 			if points.is_empty():
 164: 				continue
 165: 
 166: 			draw_entries.append({
 167: 				"kind": "pickup",
 168: 				"id": shape_id,
 169: 				"points": points,
 170: 			})
 171: 
 172: 	overlay.set_hitbox_entries(draw_entries)
 173: 
 174: 
 175: func _shape_definition_visual_points(shape_definition: Dictionary, x: float, y: float, rotation: float, scale: float) -> PackedVector2Array:
 176: 	if world_sync == null:
 177: 		return PackedVector2Array()
 178: 
 179: 	var points_value = shape_definition.get("points", [])
 180: 	if !(points_value is Array):
 181: 		return PackedVector2Array()
 182: 
 183: 	var draw_points := PackedVector2Array()
 184: 	for point_value in points_value:
 185: 		if !(point_value is Dictionary):
 186: 			continue
 187: 
 188: 		var point_x_value = point_value.get("x", null)
 189: 		var point_y_value = point_value.get("y", null)
 190: 		if point_x_value == null || point_y_value == null:
 191: 			continue
 192: 
 193: 		var local_point := Vector2(float(point_x_value), float(point_y_value))
 194: 		local_point *= scale
 195: 		local_point = local_point.rotated(rotation)
 196: 		var server_position := Vector2(x, y) + local_point
 197: 		draw_points.append(world_sync.visual_position_for_server_position(server_position))
 198: 
 199: 	return draw_points

  11: 
  12: func TestControlCollisionBodiesByKindGroupsAuthoritativeCollisionBodies(t *testing.T) {
  13: 	gameInstance := New()
  14: 	gameInstance.collisionShapes = physics.CollisionShapeCatalog{
  15: 		Bullet:  physics.ImportedCollisionShape{Name: "bullet", Type: "circle", Radius: 3},
  16: 		Pickups: map[string]physics.ImportedCollisionShape{"powerup": {Name: "powerup", Type: "circle", Radius: 3}},
  17: 		Ship:    physics.ImportedCollisionShape{Name: "ship", Type: "rectangle", Size: []float64{4, 2}},
  18: 	}
  19: 
  20: 	gameInstance.entities.Players["player-1"] = &runtime.Ship{ID: "player-1", X: 10, Y: 20, Rotation: math.Pi / 2}
  21: 	gameInstance.entities.Projectiles["bullet-1"] = &runtime.Bullet{ID: "bullet-1", X: 1, Y: 2, Rotation: 0}
  22: 	gameInstance.entities.Asteroids["asteroid-1"] = &runtime.Asteroid{ID: "asteroid-1", X: 30, Y: 40, Size: 2, Variant: 0}
  23: 	gameInstance.entities.Pickups["pickup-1"] = &pickups.Pickup{ID: "pickup-1", Type: pickups.TypeOneUp, X: -2, Y: 5}
  24: 	gameInstance.entities.Pickups["pickup-skipped"] = &pickups.Pickup{ID: "pickup-skipped", Type: pickups.TypeTorpedo, X: 0, Y: 0}
  25: 
  26: 	control := NewControl(gameInstance)
  27: 	bodies := control.CollisionBodiesByKind()
  28: 
  29: 	if got := len(bodies["player"]); got != 1 {
  30: 		t.Fatalf("expected 1 player body, got %d", got)
  31: 	}
  32: 	if got := len(bodies["bullet"]); got != 1 {
  33: 		t.Fatalf("expected 1 bullet body, got %d", got)
  34: 	}
  35: 	if got := len(bodies["pickup"]); got != 1 {
  36: 		t.Fatalf("expected 1 pickup body, got %d", got)
  37: 	}
  38: 	if got := len(bodies["asteroid"]); got != 0 {
  39: 		t.Fatalf("expected asteroid collision body to be skipped, got %d", got)
  40: 	}
  41: 
  42: 	if got := bodies["player"][0].ID; got != "player-1" {
  43: 		t.Fatalf("expected player id %q, got %q", "player-1", got)
  44: 	}
  45: 	if got := bodies["player"][0].Shape.Type; got != "rectangle" {
  46: 		t.Fatalf("expected player shape %q, got %q", "rectangle", got)
  47: 	}
  48: 	if got := bodies["player"][0].Shape.Size; got.X != 4 || got.Y != 2 {
  49: 		t.Fatalf("expected player rectangle size (4, 2), got %+v", got)
  50: 	}
  51: }

  26: 				Points: [][]float64{{-1, -1}, {1, -1}, {0, 1}},

  28: 		},

  46: func TestBuildShapeCatalogSkipsInvalidShapes(t *testing.T) {

  48: 		Bullet: physics.ImportedCollisionShape{
  49: 			Name: "invalid-bullet",
  50: 			Type: "circle",

  53: 
  54: 	got := BuildShapeCatalog(catalog)

  60: 
  61: func assertShapeEntry(t *testing.T, got map[string]DebugShapeDefinition, id string, kind string) {
  62: 	t.Helper()
  63: 
  64: 	entry, ok := got[id]
  65: 	if !ok {
  66: 		t.Fatalf("BuildShapeCatalog() missing %q", id)
  67: 	}
  68: 	if entry.ID != id {
  69: 		t.Fatalf("entry.ID = %q, want %q", entry.ID, id)
  70: 	}
  71: 	if entry.Kind != kind {
  72: 		t.Fatalf("entry.Kind = %q, want %q", entry.Kind, kind)
  73: 	}
  74: 	if len(entry.Points) == 0 {
  75: 		t.Fatalf("entry.Points is empty for %q", id)
  76: 	}
  77: 	if strings.Contains(entry.ID, "player-") || strings.Contains(entry.ID, "asteroid-") || strings.Contains(entry.ID, "pickup-") {
  78: 		t.Fatalf("entry.ID %q unexpectedly uses a live entity id format", entry.ID)
  79: 	}
  80: 	for _, point := range entry.Points {
  81: 		if point.X == 0 && point.Y == 0 {
  82: 			t.Fatalf("entry.Points for %q unexpectedly contains origin position", id)
  83: 		}
  84: 	}
  85: }

  74: 	var tree := view_anchor.get_tree()
  75: 	return tree.get_node_count() if tree != null else -1

  92: func apply_world_lane_state(world_lane_state_ref) -> void:
  93: 	if world_lane_state_ref == null:
  94: 		return
  95: 
  96: 	world_lane_state = world_lane_state_ref
  97: 	if player_render_api != null:
  98: 		player_render_api.remove_missing(world_lane_state.ships, current_self_id)
  99: 		player_render_api.apply_state(current_self_id, world_lane_state.ships)
 100: 	var local_visual_position: Vector2 = player_render_api.visual_position()
 101: 	var local_server_position: Vector2 = player_render_api.server_position()
 102: 	if projectile_sync != null:
 103: 		if world_lane_state.bullet_full_sync_required:
 104: 			projectile_sync.remove_missing(world_lane_state.bullets)
 105: 			projectile_sync.apply(world_lane_state.bullets, local_visual_position, local_server_position)
 106: 			world_lane_state.clear_bullet_change_sets()
 107: 		else:
 108: 			for bullet_id in world_lane_state.removed_bullet_ids.keys():
 109: 				projectile_sync.remove_projectile(str(bullet_id))
 110: 			for bullet_id in world_lane_state.dirty_bullet_ids.keys():
 111: 				if world_lane_state.bullets.has(bullet_id):
 112: 					projectile_sync.apply_projectile(str(bullet_id), world_lane_state.bullets[bullet_id], local_visual_position, local_server_position)
 113: 			world_lane_state.clear_bullet_change_sets()
 114: 	if asteroid_sync != null:
 115: 		if world_lane_state.asteroid_full_sync_required:
 116: 			asteroid_sync.remove_missing(world_lane_state.asteroids)
 117: 			asteroid_sync.apply(world_lane_state.asteroids, player_render_api.visual_position(), player_render_api.server_position())
 118: 			world_lane_state.clear_asteroid_change_sets()
 119: 		else:
 120: 			for asteroid_id in world_lane_state.removed_asteroid_ids.keys():
 121: 				asteroid_sync.remove_asteroid(str(asteroid_id))
 122: 			for asteroid_id in world_lane_state.dirty_asteroid_ids.keys():
 123: 				if world_lane_state.asteroids.has(asteroid_id):
 124: 					asteroid_sync.apply_asteroid(str(asteroid_id), world_lane_state.asteroids[asteroid_id], player_render_api.visual_position(), player_render_api.server_position())
 125: 			world_lane_state.clear_asteroid_change_sets()
 126: 	if pickup_sync != null:
 127: 		pickup_sync.remove_missing(world_lane_state.pickups)
 128: 		pickup_sync.apply(world_lane_state.pickups, player_render_api.visual_position(), player_render_api.server_position())

 145: func apply_state(
 146: 	self_id: String,
 147: 	server_players: Dictionary,
 148: 	server_bullets: Dictionary,
 149: 	server_asteroids: Dictionary,
 150: 	server_pickups: Dictionary = {}

 160: 	player_render_api.apply_state(self_id, server_players)
 161: 	projectile_sync.apply(

 163: 		player_render_api.visual_position(),
 164: 		player_render_api.server_position()

 167: 		server_asteroids,
 168: 		player_render_api.visual_position(),
 169: 		player_render_api.server_position()
 170: 	)
 171: 	pickup_sync.apply(
 172: 		server_pickups,
 173: 		player_render_api.visual_position(),
 174: 		player_render_api.server_position()
 175: 	)

 180: 	player_render_api.interpolate(weight, current_self_id)
 181: 	projectile_sync.interpolate(weight)
 182: 	asteroid_sync.interpolate(weight)
 183: 	pickup_sync.interpolate(weight)

  18: func TestHandleGameplayPacketRoutesClientConfigToGameHandlePacket(t *testing.T) {
  19: 	const playerID = "player-1"
  20: 
  21: 	gameInstance := game.New()
  22: 	spawnPosition := physics.Vector2{X: 320, Y: 240}
  23: 	control := game.NewControl(gameInstance)
  24: 	if !control.EnsurePlayerSession(playerID, spawnPosition) {
  25: 		t.Fatal("expected EnsurePlayerSession to succeed")
  26: 	}
  27: 	if !control.SpawnPlayerShip(playerID, spawnPosition, runtime.ClientConfig{
  28: 		VisibleWorldWidth:  1920,
  29: 		VisibleWorldHeight: 1080,
  30: 	}) {
  31: 		t.Fatal("expected SpawnPlayerShip to succeed")
  32: 	}
  33: 
  34: 	session := &webSocketSession{
  35: 		context: SessionContext{Room: rooms.NewRoom("room-1", rooms.RoomStateInGame, gameInstance), RoomID: "room-1", GamePlayerID: playerID},
  36: 	}
  37: 
  38: 	packet := game.ClientPacket{
  39: 		Type: game.PacketTypeClientConfig,
  40: 		Config: runtime.ClientConfig{
  41: 			VisibleWorldWidth:  640,
  42: 			VisibleWorldHeight: 360,
  43: 		},
  44: 	}
  45: 
  46: 	if !inbound.HandleGameplayPacket(gameplayPacketTestAdapter{session: session}, packet) {
  47: 		t.Fatal("expected client_config packet to be handled")
  48: 	}
  49: 
  50: 	cameraConfig := cameraViewConfigForPlayer(t, gameInstance, playerID)
  51: 	if cameraConfig.VisibleWorldWidth != packet.Config.VisibleWorldWidth {
  52: 		t.Fatalf("expected camera width %v, got %v", packet.Config.VisibleWorldWidth, cameraConfig.VisibleWorldWidth)
  53: 	}
  54: 	if cameraConfig.VisibleWorldHeight != packet.Config.VisibleWorldHeight {
  55: 		t.Fatalf("expected camera height %v, got %v", packet.Config.VisibleWorldHeight, cameraConfig.VisibleWorldHeight)
  56: 	}
  57: 
  58: 	sessionConfig := playerSessionConfigForPlayer(t, gameInstance, playerID)
  59: 	if sessionConfig.VisibleWorldWidth != packet.Config.VisibleWorldWidth {
  60: 		t.Fatalf("expected session width %v, got %v", packet.Config.VisibleWorldWidth, sessionConfig.VisibleWorldWidth)
  61: 	}
  62: 	if sessionConfig.VisibleWorldHeight != packet.Config.VisibleWorldHeight {
  63: 		t.Fatalf("expected session height %v, got %v", packet.Config.VisibleWorldHeight, sessionConfig.VisibleWorldHeight)
  64: 	}
  65: }

 124: func cameraViewConfigForPlayer(t *testing.T, gameInstance *game.Game, playerID string) runtime.ClientConfig {
 125: 	t.Helper()
 126: 
 127: 	cameraViews := exportedFieldValue(t, gameInstance, "cameraViews")
 128: 	cameraView := mapValueForKey(t, cameraViews, playerID)
 129: 	if !cameraView.IsValid() {
 130: 		t.Fatalf("expected camera view for %q", playerID)
 131: 	}
 132: 
 133: 	return clientConfigFieldValue(t, cameraView)
 134: }

 136: func playerSessionConfigForPlayer(t *testing.T, gameInstance *game.Game, playerID string) runtime.ClientConfig {
 137: 	t.Helper()
 138: 
 139: 	playerSessions := exportedFieldValue(t, gameInstance, "playerSessions")
 140: 	session := mapValueForKey(t, playerSessions, playerID)

 142: 		t.Fatalf("expected player session for %q", playerID)
 143: 	}
 144: 
 145: 	return clientConfigFieldValue(t, session)

 147: 
 148: func exportedFieldValue(t *testing.T, target any, fieldName string) reflect.Value {
 149: 	t.Helper()
 150: 
 151: 	field := reflect.ValueOf(target).Elem().FieldByName(fieldName)
 152: 	return reflect.NewAt(field.Type(), unsafe.Pointer(field.UnsafeAddr())).Elem()
 153: }
 154: 
 155: func mapValueForKey(t *testing.T, mapValue reflect.Value, key string) reflect.Value {
 156: 	t.Helper()
 157: 
 158: 	value := mapValue.MapIndex(reflect.ValueOf(key))
 159: 	if !value.IsValid() {
 160: 		return reflect.Value{}
 161: 	}
 162: 
 163: 	return value
 164: }
 165: 
 166: func clientConfigFieldValue(t *testing.T, value reflect.Value) runtime.ClientConfig {
 167: 	t.Helper()
 168: 
 169: 	if value.Kind() == reflect.Pointer {
 170: 		value = value.Elem()
 171: 	}
 172: 	field := value.FieldByName("Config")
 173: 	return reflect.NewAt(field.Type(), unsafe.Pointer(field.UnsafeAddr())).Elem().Interface().(runtime.ClientConfig)
 174: }

 191:

   1: package outbound

   4: 	"github.com/Lokee86/space-rocks/services/game-server/internal/devtools"

   8: 
   9: func CanSendDebugStatus(room *rooms.Room) bool {
  10: 	if room == nil {
  11: 		return false
  12: 	}
  13: 	context := room.GameplayContext()
  14: 	return context.Game != nil &&
  15: 		devtools.Enabled() &&
  16: 		(context.State == rooms.RoomStateInGame || context.State == rooms.RoomStateGameOver)
  17: }
  18: 
  19: func BuildDebugStatusPacket(room *rooms.Room, playerID string, requestID string) (devtools.DebugStatusPacket, bool) {
  20: 	if !CanSendDebugStatus(room) {
  21: 		return devtools.DebugStatusPacket{}, false
  22: 	}
  23: 	context := room.GameplayContext()
  24: 	control := game.NewControl(context.Game)
  25: 	controller := devtools.NewController(devtools.Dependencies{Target: control})
  26: 	return devtools.DebugStatusPacket{
  27: 		Type:          devtools.PacketTypeDebugStatus,
  28: 		RequestID:     requestID,
  29: 		DebugStatus:   controller.StatusFor(playerID),
  30: 		DebugStatuses: controller.StatusesForAllPlayers(),
  31: 	}, true
  32: }

   1: package devtools

   3: import streamruntime "github.com/Lokee86/space-rocks/services/game-server/internal/devtools/streamruntime"
   4: 
   5: type Dependencies struct {
   6: 	Target           Target
   7: 	Streams          *streamruntime.Runtime
   8: 	ObserverRegistry *ObserverRegistry
   9: }
  10: 
  11: type Controller struct {
  12: 	target           Target
  13: 	streams          *streamruntime.Runtime
  14: 	observerRegistry *ObserverRegistry
  15: }
  16: 
  17: var defaultObserverRegistry = NewObserverRegistry()
  18: 
  19: func NewController(deps Dependencies) *Controller {
  20: 	streams := deps.Streams
  21: 	if streams == nil {
  22: 		streams = streamruntime.DefaultRuntime
  23: 	}
  24: 
  25: 	observerRegistry := deps.ObserverRegistry
  26: 	if observerRegistry == nil {
  27: 		observerRegistry = defaultObserverRegistry
  28: 	}
  29: 
  30: 	return &Controller{
  31: 		target:           deps.Target,
  32: 		streams:          streams,
  33: 		observerRegistry: observerRegistry,
  34: 	}
  35: }
  36: 
  37: func (controller *Controller) HandleCommand(playerID string, command DebugCommand) bool {
  38: 	if controller == nil || controller.target == nil {
  39: 		return false
  40: 	}
  41: 
  42: 	switch command.Type {
  43: 	case PacketTypeToggleDebugInvincible:
  44: 		return handleToggleDebugInvincible(controller.target, playerID, command)
  45: 	case PacketTypeToggleDebugInfiniteLives:
  46: 		return handleToggleDebugInfiniteLives(controller.target, playerID, command)
  47: 	case PacketTypeToggleDebugFreezeWorld:
  48: 		return handleToggleDebugFreezeWorld(controller.target, playerID, command)
  49: 	case PacketTypeToggleDebugFreezePlayer:
  50: 		return handleToggleDebugFreezePlayer(controller.target, playerID, command)
  51: 	case PacketTypeDebugKillPlayer:
  52: 		return handleDebugKillPlayer(controller.target, playerID, command)
  53: 	case PacketTypeDebugSpawnEntity:
  54: 		return handleDebugSpawnEntity(controller.target, playerID, command)
  55: 	case PacketTypeDebugSpawnPickup:
  56: 		return handleDebugSpawnPickup(controller.target, playerID, command)
  57: 	case PacketTypeDebugBeginContinuousBulletStream:
  58: 		return handleDebugBeginContinuousBulletStream(controller, playerID, command)
  59: 	case PacketTypeDebugRespawnPlayer:
  60: 		return handleDebugRespawnPlayer(controller.target, playerID, command)
  61: 	case PacketTypeDebugSetScore:
  62: 		return handleDebugSetScore(controller.target, playerID, command)
  63: 	case PacketTypeDebugAddScore:
  64: 		return handleDebugAddScore(controller.target, playerID, command)
  65: 	case PacketTypeDebugSetLives:
  66: 		return handleDebugSetLives(controller.target, playerID, command)
  67: 	case PacketTypeDebugAddLives:
  68: 		return handleDebugAddLives(controller.target, playerID, command)
  69: 	case PacketTypeDebugClearBullets:
  70: 		return handleDebugClearBullets(controller, playerID, command)
  71: 	case PacketTypeDebugClearAsteroids:
  72: 		return handleDebugClearAsteroids(controller.target, playerID, command)
  73: 	default:
  74: 		return false
  75: 	}
  76: }
  77: 
  78: func (controller *Controller) StatusFor(playerID string) DebugStatus {
  79: 	status := DebugStatus{
  80: 		WorldFrozen:      controller.target.WorldFrozen(),
  81: 		AsteroidsFrozen:  controller.target.AsteroidsFrozen(),
  82: 		BulletsFrozen:    controller.target.BulletsFrozen(),
  83: 		SpawningFrozen:   controller.target.SpawningFrozen(),
  84: 		CollisionsFrozen: controller.target.CollisionsFrozen(),
  85: 	}
  86: 
  87: 	if invincible, ok := controller.target.PlayerInvincible(playerID); ok {
  88: 		status.Invincible = invincible
  89: 	}
  90: 	if infiniteLives, ok := controller.target.InfiniteLives(playerID); ok {
  91: 		status.InfiniteLives = infiniteLives
  92: 	}
  93: 	if playerFrozen, ok := controller.target.PlayerFrozen(playerID); ok {
  94: 		status.PlayerFrozen = playerFrozen
  95: 	}
  96: 
  97: 	return status
  98: }
  99: 
 100: func (controller *Controller) StatusesForAllPlayers() map[string]DebugStatus {
 101: 	statuses := make(map[string]DebugStatus)
 102: 	if controller == nil || controller.target == nil {
 103: 		return statuses
 104: 	}
 105: 
 106: 	decision := controller.target.MatchDecision()
 107: 	for _, player := range decision.Players {
 108: 		statuses[player.ID] = controller.StatusFor(player.ID)
 109: 	}
 110: 	return statuses
 111: }

--- 344-356 ---

===== docs/devtools/design/devtools-packet-protocol.md =====
--- 320-332 ---
 320: ## Server command routing
 321: 
 322: Runtime debug commands no longer enter through the game-server WebSocket read loop. The `sr.tooling` route applies command policy before decoding the debug command.
 323: 
 324: Current runtime command routing order is:
 325: 
 326: ```text
 327: client command payload with request_id and trace_id
 328: sr.tooling
 329: tooling packet policy and capability preflight
 330: room attachment check
 331: decode into devtools.DebugCommand
 332: existing devtools Controller.HandleCommand
--- 370-382 ---
 370: Devtools commands do not route through normal `Game.HandlePacket` gameplay packet handling.
 371: 
 372: ## Server output packets
 373: 
 374: ### Debug status
 375: 
 376: `debug_status` reports authoritative debug-control state through a connection-local `sr.tooling` subscription.
 377: 
 378: ```text
 379: debug_status_subscribe(request_id)
 380: -> first eligible tooling-router tick emits immediately
 381: -> later pushes occur every eight tooling-router ticks
 382: -> debug_status.request_id echoes the subscription request ID
--- 372-384 ---
 372: ## Server output packets
 373: 
 374: ### Debug status
 375: 
 376: `debug_status` reports authoritative debug-control state through a connection-local `sr.tooling` subscription.
 377: 
 378: ```text
 379: debug_status_subscribe(request_id)
 380: -> first eligible tooling-router tick emits immediately
 381: -> later pushes occur every eight tooling-router ticks
 382: -> debug_status.request_id echoes the subscription request ID
 383: 
 384: debug_status_unsubscribe(request_id)
--- 450-462 ---
 450: 
 451: Runtime client devtools commands use the tooling outbound path:
 452: 
 453: ```text
 454: devtools hotkey or window action
 455: -> devtools command, placement, or overlay context
 456: -> GameplayDebugFlow or DevConnectionService builds request_id/trace_id payload
 457: -> ClientConnectionService.send_tooling_packet(packet)
 458: -> sr.tooling
 459: ```
 460: 
 461: The server networking/tooling route capability-checks commands and readout requests. `ToolingPacketRouter` receives correlated command results/errors plus `debug_status` and `debug_shape_catalog`. Continuous telemetry/ping remains the next migration slice.
 462: 
--- 480-492 ---
 480: 
 481: No client-side gate should be treated as the authority boundary for gameplay-affecting devtools behavior.
 482: 
 483: ## Relationship to real gameplay seams
 484: 
 485: Devtools packets route into real gameplay seams; they do not create alternate gameplay systems.
 486: 
 487: Required boundary rules:
 488: 
 489: ```text
 490: devtools command packets stay out of generated game packet structs
 491: normal gameplay packets stay out of devtools command ownership
 492: server devtools handlers own debug command dispatch

  86: ## Server authority
  87: 
  88: The server owns all gameplay mutation behind player-targeted devtools commands.
  89: 
  90: The client may request:
  91: 
  92: ```text
  93: make a player invincible
  94: give a player infinite lives
  95: freeze a player
  96: kill a player
  97: respawn a player
  98: set or add score
--- 128-140 ---
 128: ## Client presentation
 129: 
 130: The server does not own target labels, dropdown rows, telemetry widgets, or window controls.
 131: 
 132: Client-side devtools presentation may expose:
 133: 
 134: ```text
 135: explicit player rows
 136: All Players rows
 137: Game Target rows
 138: local player fallback behavior
 139: target telemetry
 140: target status labels

 154: 		t.Fatal("expected invincible player not to be marked for despawn")
 155: 	}

 161: 	}

 223: func TestDebugInfiniteLivesPlayerDiesWithoutLosingLife(t *testing.T) {
 224: 	scenario := newScenario(t)
 225: 	scenario.useCircleCollisionShapes()
 226: 	playerID := scenario.addPlayer()
 227: 	player := scenario.playerState(playerID, playerID)
 228: 	scenario.placeAsteroid("asteroid-1", physics.Vector2{X: player.X, Y: player.Y}, 1)
 229: 
 230: 	devtools.HandleCommand(scenario.control, playerID, devtools.DebugCommand{
 231: 		Type: devtools.PacketTypeToggleDebugInfiniteLives,
 232: 	})
 233: 	scenario.step(1.0 / float64(constants.ServerTickRate))
 234: 
 235: 	if !scenario.playerPendingDespawn(playerID) {
 236: 		t.Fatal("expected infinite-lives player to still die and despawn")
 237: 	}
 238: 	snapshot := scenario.presentationSnapshot(playerID)
 239: 	if snapshot.Lives != constants.PlayerStartingLives {
 240: 		t.Fatalf("expected infinite-lives player to keep %d lives, got %d", constants.PlayerStartingLives, snapshot.Lives)
 241: 	}
 242: 	if countPendingEventsOfType(snapshot.PendingEvents, servergame.PacketTypeShipDeath) != 1 {
 243: 		t.Fatalf("expected death event for infinite-lives player, got %d", countPendingEventsOfType(snapshot.PendingEvents, servergame.PacketTypeShipDeath))
 244: 	}
 245: 	var deathEvent *servergame.EventState
 246: 	for i := range snapshot.PendingEvents {
 247: 		if snapshot.PendingEvents[i].Event.Type == servergame.PacketTypeShipDeath {
 248: 			deathEvent = &snapshot.PendingEvents[i].Event
 249: 			break
 250: 		}
 251: 	}
 252: 	if deathEvent == nil {
 253: 		t.Fatal("expected ship_death event in gameplay snapshot")
 254: 	}
 255: 	if deathEvent.Lives != constants.PlayerStartingLives {
 256: 		t.Fatalf("expected death event to keep %d lives, got %d", constants.PlayerStartingLives, deathEvent.Lives)
 257: 	}
 258: 	foundDamageApplied := false
 259: 	for _, pending := range snapshot.PendingEvents {
 260: 		if pending.Event.Type == "damage_applied" {
 261: 			foundDamageApplied = true
 262: 			break
 263: 		}
 264: 	}
 265: 	if !foundDamageApplied {
 266: 		t.Fatal("expected damage_applied event for infinite-lives player death")
 267: 	}
 268: 
 269: 	scenario.step(constants.CollisionDespawnDelay)
 270: 	scenario.advanceRespawnTimer(playerID, constants.PlayerRespawnDelay)
 271: 	scenario.send(playerID, servergame.ClientPacket{Type: servergame.PacketTypeRespawn})
 272: 
 273: 	if !scenario.playerInfiniteLives(playerID) {
 274: 		t.Fatal("expected infinite lives flag to persist after respawn")
 275: 	}
 276: }

 722: func TestDebugFrozenWorldDoesNotRunBulletAsteroidCollisionsOrScore(t *testing.T) {
 723: 	scenario := newScenario(t)
 724: 	scenario.useCircleCollisionShapes()
 725: 	playerID := scenario.addPlayer()
 726: 	player := scenario.playerState(playerID, playerID)
 727: 	position := physics.Vector2{X: player.X, Y: player.Y}
 728: 	scenario.placeBullet("bullet-1", playerID, position, physics.Vector2{})
 729: 	scenario.placeAsteroid("asteroid-1", position, 1)
 730: 
 731: 	devtools.HandleCommand(scenario.control, playerID, devtools.DebugCommand{
 732: 		Type: devtools.PacketTypeToggleDebugFreezeWorld,
 733: 	})
 734: 	scenario.step(1.0 / float64(constants.ServerTickRate))
 735: 
 736: 	if scenario.bulletPendingDespawn("bullet-1") {
 737: 		t.Fatal("expected frozen world not to mark colliding bullet for despawn")
 738: 	}
 739: 	if scenario.asteroidPendingDespawn("asteroid-1") {
 740: 		t.Fatal("expected frozen world not to mark hit asteroid for despawn")
 741: 	}
 742: 	if session := scenario.playerSessionState(playerID, playerID); session.Score != 0 {

 753: func TestDebugKillPlayerMarksDespawnQueuesDeathAndReducesLives(t *testing.T) {
 754: 	scenario := newScenario(t)
 755: 	playerID := scenario.addPlayer()
 756: 
 757: 	devtools.HandleCommand(scenario.control, playerID, devtools.DebugCommand{
 758: 		Type: devtools.PacketTypeDebugKillPlayer,
 759: 	})
 760: 
 761: 	if !scenario.playerPendingDespawn(playerID) {
 762: 		t.Fatal("expected debug kill to mark player pending despawn")
 763: 	}
 764: 	if events := scenario.pendingEventCount(playerID); events != 1 {
 765: 		t.Fatalf("expected one queued ship death event, got %d", events)
 766: 	}
 767: 	snapshot := scenario.presentationSnapshot(playerID)
 768: 	if countPendingEventsOfType(snapshot.PendingEvents, servergame.PacketTypeShipDeath) != 1 {
 769: 		t.Fatalf("expected one ship death event in packet, got %d", countPendingEventsOfType(snapshot.PendingEvents, servergame.PacketTypeShipDeath))
 770: 	}
 771: 	var deathEvent *servergame.EventState
 772: 	for i := range snapshot.PendingEvents {
 773: 		if snapshot.PendingEvents[i].Event.Type == servergame.PacketTypeShipDeath {
 774: 			deathEvent = &snapshot.PendingEvents[i].Event
 775: 			break
 776: 		}
 777: 	}
 778: 	if deathEvent == nil {
 779: 		t.Fatal("expected ship death event in packet")
 780: 	}
 781: 	expectedLives := constants.PlayerStartingLives - 1
 782: 	if snapshot.Lives != expectedLives {
 783: 		t.Fatalf("expected debug kill to reduce lives to %d, got %d", expectedLives, snapshot.Lives)
 784: 	}
 785: }

 787: func TestDebugKillPlayerCanKillAnotherActivePlayer(t *testing.T) {
 788: 	scenario := newScenario(t)
 789: 	playerA := scenario.addPlayer()
 790: 	playerB := scenario.addPlayer()
 791: 
 792: 	devtools.HandleCommand(scenario.control, playerA, devtools.DebugCommand{
 793: 		Type:           devtools.PacketTypeDebugKillPlayer,
 794: 		TargetPlayerID: playerB,
 795: 	})
 796: 
 797: 	if scenario.playerPendingDespawn(playerA) {
 798: 		t.Fatal("expected source player to remain active")
 799: 	}
 800: 	if !scenario.playerPendingDespawn(playerB) {
 801: 		t.Fatal("expected target player to be marked pending despawn")
 802: 	}
 803: 	snapshotA := scenario.presentationSnapshot(playerA)
 804: 	if len(snapshotA.PendingEvents) != 1 {
 805: 		t.Fatalf("expected one ship death event in source view, got %d", len(snapshotA.PendingEvents))
 806: 	}
 807: 	if snapshotA.PendingEvents[0].Event.Type != servergame.PacketTypeShipDeath {
 808: 		t.Fatalf("expected ship death event type %q, got %q", servergame.PacketTypeShipDeath, snapshotA.PendingEvents[0].Event.Type)
 809: 	}
 810: 	if snapshotA.PendingEvents[0].Event.PlayerID != playerB {
 811: 		t.Fatalf("expected ship death event player id %q, got %q", playerB, snapshotA.PendingEvents[0].Event.PlayerID)
 812: 	}
 813: 	snapshotB := scenario.presentationSnapshot(playerB)
 814: 	expectedLives := constants.PlayerStartingLives - 1
 815: 	if snapshotB.Lives != expectedLives {
 816: 		t.Fatalf("expected target debug kill to reduce lives to %d, got %d", expectedLives, snapshotB.Lives)
 817: 	}
 818: }

 959: func TestDebugRespawnPlayerAllPlayersRespawnsEligiblePlayersAndIgnoresActivePlayers(t *testing.T) {
 960: 	scenario := newScenario(t)
 961: 	activePlayerID := scenario.addPlayer()
 962: 	respawnEligiblePlayerID := scenario.addPlayer()
 963: 	activePlayerBefore := scenario.playerState(activePlayerID, activePlayerID)
 964: 	respawnPosition := physics.Vector2{X: 321, Y: 654}
 965: 
 966: 	scenario.removePlayerEntity(respawnEligiblePlayerID)
 967: 	scenario.setSessionSpawnPosition(respawnEligiblePlayerID, respawnPosition)
 968: 
 969: 	devtools.HandleCommand(scenario.control, activePlayerID, devtools.DebugCommand{
 970: 		Type:        devtools.PacketTypeDebugRespawnPlayer,
 971: 		TargetScope: "all_players",
 972: 	})
 973: 
 974: 	if !scenario.playerEntityExists(respawnEligiblePlayerID) {
 975: 		t.Fatal("expected all-players debug respawn to recreate the eligible player entity")
 976: 	}
 977: 	respawned := scenario.playerState(activePlayerID, respawnEligiblePlayerID)
 978: 	if respawned.X != respawnPosition.X || respawned.Y != respawnPosition.Y {
 979: 		t.Fatalf(
 980: 			"expected eligible player to respawn at (%v, %v), got (%v, %v)",
 981: 			respawnPosition.X,
 982: 			respawnPosition.Y,
 983: 			respawned.X,
 984: 			respawned.Y,
 985: 		)
 986: 	}
 987: 
 988: 	activePlayerAfter := scenario.playerState(activePlayerID, activePlayerID)
 989: 	if activePlayerAfter.X != activePlayerBefore.X || activePlayerAfter.Y != activePlayerBefore.Y {
 990: 		t.Fatalf(
 991: 			"expected active player to be ignored by respawn guard and stay at (%v, %v), got (%v, %v)",
 992: 			activePlayerBefore.X,
 993: 			activePlayerBefore.Y,
 994: 			activePlayerAfter.X,
 995: 			activePlayerAfter.Y,
 996: 		)
 997: 	}
 998: }

  35: func connect_connection_signals() -> void:
  36: 	_connect_connection_signal("connected", Callable(self, "_on_connection_connected"))
  37: 	_connect_connection_signal("closed", Callable(self, "_on_connection_closed"))
  38: 	_connect_connection_signal("websocket_auth_result_received", Callable(self, "_on_websocket_auth_result_received"))
  39: 	_connect_connection_signal("realtime_transport_ready", Callable(self, "_on_realtime_transport_ready"))
  40:

  42: func connect_room_signals() -> void:
  43: 	_connect_connection_signal("room_snapshot_received", Callable(self, "_on_room_snapshot_received"))
  44: 	_connect_connection_signal("room_state_changed", Callable(self, "_on_room_state_changed"))
  45: 	_connect_connection_signal("room_error_received", Callable(self, "_on_room_error_received"))
  46: 
  47: 
  48: func connect_gameplay_signals() -> void:

  50: 	_connect_connection_signal("debug_status_received", Callable(self, "_on_debug_status_received"))
  51: 	_connect_connection_signal("player_pause_state_received", Callable(self, "_on_player_pause_state_received"))

  53: 
  54: func _connect_connection_signal(signal_name: StringName, handler: Callable) -> void:
  55: 	if connection_service == null:
  56: 		return
  57: 	if !connection_service.has_signal(signal_name):
  58: 		return
  59: 	if connection_service.is_connected(signal_name, handler):
  60: 		return
  61: 
  62: 	connection_service.connect(signal_name, handler)
  63: 
  64: 
  65: func _on_connection_connected() -> void:
  66: 	_webrtc_gameplay_ready = false
  67: 	_try_send_pending_boot_request()
  68: 
  69: 
  70: func _on_connection_closed() -> void:
  71: 	_coordinated_active_match_id = ""
  72: 	if gameplay_session_controller != null:
  73: 		gameplay_session_controller.reset()
  74: 	if connection_service != null:
  75: 		connection_service.end_realtime_match()
  76: 
  77: 
  78: 
  79: 
  80: 
  81: func _on_realtime_transport_ready() -> void:
  82: 	_webrtc_gameplay_ready = true
  83: 	_try_send_pending_boot_request()
  84: 
  85: 
  86: func _try_send_pending_boot_request() -> void:
  87: 	if shell_boot_flow == null:
  88: 		return
  89: 	if !_webrtc_gameplay_ready:
  90: 		return
  91: 
  92: 	if shell_boot_flow.pending_request_is_single_player():
  93: 		shell_boot_flow.send_pending_boot_request()
  94: 		return
  95: 
  96: 	if shell_boot_flow.pending_request_is_multiplayer():
  97: 		if connection_service != null && connection_service.is_websocket_auth_authenticated():
  98: 			shell_boot_flow.send_pending_boot_request()
  99: 
 100: 
 101: func _on_websocket_auth_result_received(packet: Dictionary) -> void:
 102: 	if shell_boot_flow == null:
 103: 		return
 104: 
 105: 	if bool(packet.get("authenticated", false)):
 106: 		_try_send_pending_boot_request()
 107: 	else:
 108: 		var error_code := str(packet.get("error_code", ""))
 109: 		if error_code == "token_verification_unavailable":
 110: 			shell_boot_flow.send_pending_boot_request()
 111: 		else:
 112: 			pass
 113:

 115: func _on_room_snapshot_received(packet: Dictionary) -> void:
 116: 	if room_session_controller == null:
 117: 		return
 118: 	room_session_controller.handle_room_snapshot(packet)
 119: 	_coordinate_room_match()
 120: 	_refresh_match_end_state()
 121: 
 122: 
 123: func _on_room_state_changed(packet: Dictionary) -> void:
 124: 	if room_session_controller == null:
 125: 		return
 126: 	room_session_controller.handle_room_state_changed(packet)
 127: 	if room_session_controller.current_room_state() == Constants.ROOM_STATE_LOBBY:
 128: 		_end_coordinated_match()
 129: 	_refresh_match_end_state()
 130: 
 131: 
 132: func _on_room_error_received(packet: Dictionary) -> void:
 133: 	if room_session_controller == null:
 134: 		return
 135: 	room_session_controller.handle_room_error(packet)
 136: 
 137: 
 138: 
 139: func _on_debug_shape_catalog_received(packet: Dictionary) -> void:
 140: 	if gameplay_session_controller == null:
 141: 		return
 142: 	gameplay_session_controller.handle_debug_shape_catalog_packet(packet)
 143: 
 144: 
 145: func _on_debug_status_received(packet: Dictionary) -> void:
 146: 	if gameplay_session_controller == null:
 147: 		return
 148: 	gameplay_session_controller.handle_debug_status_packet(packet)
 149: 
 150: 
 151: func _on_player_pause_state_received(packet: Dictionary) -> void:
 152: 	if gameplay_session_controller == null:
 153: 		return
 154: 	gameplay_session_controller.handle_player_pause_state(packet)
 155: 
 156: 
 157: func _coordinate_room_match() -> void:
 158: 	var room_state: String = room_session_controller.current_room_state()
 159: 	var match_id: String = room_session_controller.current_match_id()
 160: 	if room_state == Constants.ROOM_STATE_LOBBY:
 161: 		_end_coordinated_match()
 162: 		return
 163: 	if room_state != Constants.ROOM_STATE_IN_GAME or match_id.is_empty():
 164: 		return
 165: 	if _coordinated_active_match_id != match_id:
 166: 		if gameplay_session_controller != null:
 167: 			gameplay_session_controller.reset()
 168: 		if connection_service != null:
 169: 			connection_service.begin_realtime_match(match_id)
 170: 		_coordinated_active_match_id = match_id
 171: 	if gameplay_session_controller != null:
 172: 		gameplay_session_controller.begin_accepting_gameplay_packets()
 173: 
 174: func _end_coordinated_match() -> void:
 175: 	if _coordinated_active_match_id.is_empty():
 176: 		return
 177: 	if gameplay_session_controller != null:
 178: 		gameplay_session_controller.reset()
 179: 	if connection_service != null:
 180: 		connection_service.end_realtime_match()
 181: 	_coordinated_active_match_id = ""
 182: 
 183: 
 184: func _refresh_match_end_state() -> void:
 185: 	if gameplay_session_controller != null:
 186: 		gameplay_session_controller.refresh_match_end_state()

  11: func TestBuildDebugShapeCatalogPacketIncludesCorrelatedShapeCatalog(t *testing.T) {
  12: 	if !devtools.Enabled() {
  13: 		t.Skip("debug shape catalog output is disabled by this build")

{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_18","type":"command_execution","command":""C:\\Program Files\\WindowsApps\\Microsoft.PowerShell_7.6.3.0_x64__8wekyb3d8bbwe\\pwsh.exe" -Command '$repo='"'D:\\"'!bin'"\\space-rocks'; "'$lines=Get-Content -LiteralPath (Join-Path $repo '"'docs/devtools/server/player-targeting-and-scopes.md'); for("'$n=86;$n -le 282;$n++){ if($n -le 115 -or ($n -ge 128 -and $n -le 154) -or ($n -ge 246 -and $n -le 282)){ '"'{0,4}: {1}' -f "'$n,$lines[$n-1] } }; for($n=388;$n -le 479;$n++){ '"'{0,4}: {1}' -f "'$n,$lines[$n-1] }'","aggregated_output":"  86: ## Server authority
  87: 
  88: The server owns all gameplay mutation behind player-targeted devtools commands.
  89: 
  90: The client may request:
  91: 
  92: ```text
  93: make a player invincible
  94: give a player infinite lives
  95: freeze a player
  96: kill a player
  97: respawn a player
  98: set or add score
  99: set or add lives
 100: spawn a player slot
 101: apply one of those operations to all players
 102: ```
 103: 
 104: The server decides:
 105: 
 106: ```text
 107: which player IDs the command resolves to
 108: whether the game instance exists
 109: whether the requesting session has a current game player ID
 110: whether a target player exists
 111: whether the command applies to session state, active avatar state, or both
 112: whether an all-player fanout has any targets
 113: whether a respawn target is dead enough to respawn
 114: whether a requested debug-spawn player ID can be normalized and reserved
 115: ```
 128: ## Client presentation
 129: 
 130: The server does not own target labels, dropdown rows, telemetry widgets, or window controls.
 131: 
 132: Client-side devtools presentation may expose:
 133: 
 134: ```text
 135: explicit player rows
 136: All Players rows
 137: Game Target rows
 138: local player fallback behavior
 139: target telemetry
 140: target status labels
 141: ```
 142: 
 143: Those are presentation and request-building concerns. The server receives only packet fields.
 144: 
 145: Important presentation boundary:
 146: 
 147: ```text
 148: All Players
 149: -> target_scope = all_players
 150: -> target_player_id = ""
 151: 
 152: Game Target for a player
 153: -> target_player_id = <player id>
 154: 
 246: 
 247: ```text
 248: player-<positive number>
 249: ```
 250: 
 251: `Player-<positive number>` is accepted and normalized to lowercase `player-<number>`.
 252: 
 253: Respawn refuses to apply when the normalized target player is already active. When accepted, the server chooses a safe respawn position and forces the player back into the game through the game-owned respawn adapter.
 254: 
 255: `debug_spawn_entity` uses `target_player_id` only when spawning a player entity. In that path, `target_player_id` is an optional requested player slot. If it is present, the server normalizes and reserves that debug player ID. If it is empty, the server allocates the first available `player-N` ID within the configured player ID range.
 256: 
 257: Player spawn does not use `target_scope`. It is a placement/spawn request, not a player-command fanout request.
 258: 
 259: World and entity-wide commands do not use player target resolution:
 260: 
 261: ```text
 262: toggle_debug_freeze_world
 263: debug_spawn_pickup
 264: debug_spawn_entity for asteroid or bullet
 265: debug_begin_continuous_bullet_stream
 266: debug_clear_bullets
 267: debug_clear_asteroids
 268: ```
 269: 
 270: `toggle_debug_freeze_world` uses `freeze_target` instead of `target_player_id`:
 271: 
 272: ```text
 273: all
 274: asteroids
 275: bullets
 276: spawning
 277: spawns
 278: collisions
 279: ```
 280: 
 281: Empty `freeze_target` means `all`.
 282: 
 388: ## Code map
 389: 
 390: Primary server devtools targeting files:
 391: 
 392: ```text
 393: services/game-server/internal/devtools/target.go
 394: services/game-server/internal/devtools/target_player_ids.go
 395: services/game-server/internal/devtools/target_scopes.go
 396: services/game-server/internal/devtools/player_ids.go
 397: ```
 398: 
 399: Player-targeted command handlers:
 400: 
 401: ```text
 402: services/game-server/internal/devtools/toggles.go
 403: services/game-server/internal/devtools/player_counters.go
 404: services/game-server/internal/devtools/respawn_handler.go
 405: services/game-server/internal/devtools/respawn_player.go
 406: services/game-server/internal/devtools/spawn_entity.go
 407: services/game-server/internal/devtools/spawn_player.go
 408: ```
 409: 
 410: Networking command routing:
 411: 
 412: ```text
 413: services/game-server/internal/networking/client_packet_router.go
 414: services/game-server/internal/networking/inbound/router.go
 415: services/game-server/internal/protocol/packetcodec/
 416: ```
 417: 
 418: Build gates:
 419: 
 420: ```text
 421: services/game-server/internal/devtools/enabled_default.go
 422: services/game-server/internal/devtools/enabled_nodevtools.go
 423: services/game-server/internal/devtools/disabled.go
 424: ```
 425: 
 426: Game-owned devtools adapters:
 427: 
 428: ```text
 429: services/game-server/internal/game/control_status.go
 430: services/game-server/internal/game/control_counters.go
 431: services/game-server/internal/game/control_toggles.go
 432: ```
 433: 
 434: Related authoritative game state and targeting files:
 435: 
 436: ```text
 437: services/game-server/internal/game/targeting.go
 438: services/game-server/internal/game/control_status.go
 439: services/game-server/internal/game/session.go
 440: services/game-server/internal/game/runtime/ship.go
 441: services/game-server/internal/game/state_packet.go
 442: ```
 443: 
 444: Packet source and generated output:
 445: 
 446: ```text
 447: shared/packets/debug.toml
 448: shared/packets/outputs.toml
 449: services/game-server/internal/devtools/packets_generated.go
 450: client/scripts/generated/networking/packets/packets.gd
 451: ```
 452: 
 453: Important non-ownership boundaries:
 454: 
 455: ```text
 456: client/scripts/devtools/
 457: client/scripts/gameplay/targeting/
 458: services/game-server/internal/game/targeting/
 459: services/game-server/internal/game/damage/
 460: services/game-server/internal/game/rules/
 461: services/game-server/internal/networking/
 462: docs/services/game-server/simulation/targeting/
 463: docs/devtools/client/
 464: docs/data/
 465: docs/protocol/
 466: ```
 467: 
 468: Client devtools owns selector presentation and packet construction.
 469: 
 470: Game targeting owns canonical `target_kind` and `target_id`.
 471: 
 472: Damage owns debug kill damage resolution.
 473: 
 474: Rules own match/player lifecycle classification.
 475: 
 476: Networking owns packet routing and session identity.
 477: 
 478: Data/protocol docs own packet schema source and generated packet expectations.
 479:

SystemExit(main())\\r\\n--- README relevant ---\\r\\n\\r\\nThis benchmark measures the quality of ordinary missing-link suggestions from the current Demon Docs pipeline. It complements, and does not modify, the positive-only recall benchmark in [`research/codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json`](../codemap-review/space-rocks-trusted-links.json).\\r\\n\\r\\n## Corpus and sampling\\r\\n\\r\\n- Corpus: Space Rocks revision `3387c94d10fdb94008f27b404098f3e0c32d911c` (10 reviewed documents, 51 trusted links).\\r\\n- Source run: all 51 trusted links were hidden with seed `space-rocks-precision-v1`; the run produced 267 unique suggestions, including 24 recovered trusted links and 243 ordinary unmatched suggestions.\\r\\n- Sample: 150 unique document-target pairs in [`space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json`](space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json).\\r\\n- Sampling: every document’s top five was retained where available; the remainder was selected deterministically with SHA-256 least-represented fill across document area, score bucket, primary evidence kind, and rank bucket.\\r\\n- Labels were assigned after inspecting the referenced document section and target file or directory. Each row records document and target references, excerpts, target kind, a target fingerprint, evidence, and a concise rationale.\\r\\n\\r\\nThe three labels are mutually exclusive: `valid_missing_link` is correct for strict precision, `plausible_but_unnecessary` is accepted only for acceptance precision, and `incorrect` is junk.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Reproduction\\r\\n\\r\\nGenerate the source suggestion report without changing Space Rocks:\\r\\n\\r\\n```text\\r\\ngo run ./cmd/ddocs codemap benchmark --repo D:\\\\!bin\\\\space-rocks --trusted-links research\\\\codemap-review\\\\space-rocks-trusted-links.json --holdout-count 51 --seed space-rocks-precision-v1 --format json --output tmp\\\\codemap-precision\\\\full-hidden.json\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nEvaluate the labeled sample:\\r\\n\\r\\n```text\\r\\ngo run ./cmd/ddocs codemap precision --benchmark research\\\\codemap-precision\\\\space-rocks-precision-benchmark.json --suggestions tmp\\\\codemap-precision\\\\full-hidden.json\\r\\n```\\r\\n\\r\\nThe evaluator verifies that every sampled pair, score/evidence payload, and rank still exists in the source report before calculating metrics.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Results\\r\\n\\r\\nEvaluation date: 2026-07-19.\\r\\n\\r\\n| metric | result |\\r\\n| --- | four document areas: data (26), devtools (41), protocol (33), and services (50). Subsystem counts are recorded in the evaluator’s JSON output and the row-level artifact.\\r\\n\\r\\n## Limitations\\r\\n\\r\\nThis is a curated sample, not an unbiased estimate over every repository path. It is drawn from a full-hidden run of the 51-link trusted corpus, so it evaluates the current evidence pipeline under that benchmark setup and does not measure recall or unseen-document behavior. Directory suggestions are retained as ordinary candidates but are judged conservatively. Labels are auditable and source-pinned, but this artifact has one review pass rather than independent inter-rater agreement. The existing recall benchmark remains the appropriate measure for recovery of known Review\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nThis directory contains the manually reviewed Space Rocks codemap subset for the missing-link suggestion benchmark.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nThe subset is deliberately document;\\\\r\\\\n- stale, wildcard, parser-artifact, or otherwise ambiguous entries are excluded rather than treated as negative examples;\\\\r\\\\n- directory links remain marked as directories so benchmarks can evaluate them separately from exact-file links.\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\nFiles:\\\\r\\\\n\\\\r\\\\n- `space-rocks-trusted-links.json` — machine-readable trusted links and excluded
